cluster_name.description: Cluster name requested by the user. Does not have to be unique. If not specified at creation, the cluster name is an empty string.

spark_version.description: The runtime version of the cluster. You can retrieve a list of available runtime versions using the spark-versions endpoint.

node_type_id.description: The node type for the worker nodes. Refer to list-node-types for available node types.

driver_node_type_id.description: The node type for the driver node. If unset, the driver node type is set as the same value as node_type_id.

num_workers.description: Number of worker nodes. For a fixed-size cluster, set this to the desired number of workers. For an autoscaling cluster, this field is ignored in favor of autoscale settings.

spark_conf.description: Map of Spark configuration key-value pairs. These are passed directly to the Spark driver and executors.

ssh_public_keys.description: SSH public keys for accessing cluster nodes

custom_tags.description: Custom tags applied to cluster resources. Databricks adds default tags in addition to any custom tags you specify.

init_scripts.description: Init scripts to run when the cluster starts

spark_env_vars.description: Map of environment variable key-value pairs for the Spark process.

enable_elastic_disk.description: If true, enable autoscaling local storage. When enabled, the amount of disk space used by the cluster auto-adjusts.

instance_pool_id.description: ID of the instance pool to use for cluster nodes. If specified, the cluster uses the instance pool for both driver and worker nodes.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the cluster policy used to create the cluster.

enable_local_disk_encryption.description: Whether to enable local disk encryption for the cluster

runtime_engine.description: The runtime engine to use. PHOTON provides optimized query execution.

data_security_mode.description: Data security mode for the cluster. Determines how data access is controlled.

single_user_name.description: The name of the single user who can execute commands on the cluster. Required when data_security_mode is SINGLE_USER.
